user_query:
I'm a martial arts instructor getting ready to reopen my dojo and I need to organize my lesson notes and send clear announcements to students.

Please work in this workspace and do the following:

1) Build the notes index and fix the configuration issue:
- Attempt to run the index builder at scripts/build_index.py without modifying any files yet. Capture the error output from this first run to out/first_run_errors.txt so we can see what failed.
- Use the error message to diagnose and fix the problem by inspecting and updating either the configuration (config/settings.json) or the script (scripts/build_index.py). Do not alter the note files in notes/.
- After fixing, re-run the script and generate out/index.json. Capture the successful run’s stdout to out/build_stdout.txt.

2) Summarize the organized knowledge:
- Using out/index.json, generate out/summary.md that contains:
  - A “Category counts” section listing each category and its note count.
  - An “All tags” section listing the unique tags found, sorted.

3) Rewrite my reopening announcement for tone, clarity, and brevity:
- Using communications/draft_announcement.txt, produce two polished outputs:
  - communications/announcement_email.txt that starts with a single line beginning with "Subject: ", followed by a concise body under 180 words. Include a clearly labeled section "Category summary:" with a bullet list (one per line starting with "- ") of "CategoryName: count" using the counts from out/index.json.
  - communications/announcement_sms.txt as a single line no longer than 320 characters that mentions reopening and includes the category counts in the format "CategoryName: count" separated by semicolons.

Notes:
- All outputs must be created under the specified paths.
- The initial error log (out/first_run_errors.txt) should reflect the unmodified first run.
- Keep changes minimal and local; don’t introduce external dependencies or use the internet. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
